premise: I emptied my $60,000 nursing pension to buy my brother a vacuum-truck business after our mom’s funeral, on his signed promise of 20% ownership and repayment, but he forged a cancellation of the note and told everyone I’d never lent a dime. I found the original promissory note in a safe deposit box, sued, and was awarded full ownership of the business—now I manage the fleet and employ my brother as a dispatcher who must wear a name tag reading ‘Forgery Client.’
